name: Buster Bailey
abstract: William C. "Buster" Bailey (July 19, 1902 – April 12, 1967) was an American jazz clarinetist.

concerts.label: Jazz Concert in Memory of Sidney Bechet

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Chace
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee does not mention Buster Bailey; the name is introduced solely by the interviewer in a hypothetical scenario about Vali.

about_quotes.speaker: Budd Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: I became the seventh man in the John Kirby band, and that was because Buster Bailey and Kirby had had an argument... you better call Budd Johnson. So Charler Shavers and Kirby called me to come down and replace Buster Bailey.

about_quotes.speaker: William Randolph "Cozy" Cole
about_quotes.evidence: It was Chu, Hot Lips Paige, George Matthews on Trombone, Buster Bailey, Horace Henderson, Lawrence Lucie, Israel Crosby and you, and that was in 1937.

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: For one week, Buster Bailey and I got together on the Kirby book which I must admit was one of the toughest that I ever had to tackle.

about_quotes.speaker: Herman Autrey
about_quotes.evidence: Doc and his band... Buster Bailey was there playing the alto... I knew all the guys in the band, because I worked with them before.

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: that’s where I met Red Allen and J.C. Higginbotham, Buster Bailey, Charlie Shavers, people like that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001
